atlas.put_in: Allen-Cahn double-well + driven mass control (drive s, leak); 'droplet'/'dissolve' not put in
emerged: a single compact bounded droplet with a thin interface (membrane) persists when driven: {'s': 1.0, 'leak': 0.0, 'inside_frac': 0.287, 'interface_band': 0.04, 'n_components': 1, 'border_touch': 0, 'alive': True, 'bounded_single_vesicle': True}
cut the drive (s=0) -> it dissolves ({'s': 0.0, 'leak': 0.0, 'inside_frac': 0.0, 'interface_band': 0.0, 'n_components': 0, 'border_touch': 0, 'alive': False, 'bounded_single_vesicle': False}) = death
surprises: a spatial vesicle with a membrane (not just kinetics) lives and dies by the same drive rule
atlas.persistence: the vesicle persists only while driven above a leak-dependent threshold
atlas.not_scripted_check: the droplet + interface + dissolution emerge from the field dynamics; no shape/death imposed
atlas.claim_tier: measured (bounded single-droplet, driven persistence, dissolution, leak->drive) ; analogy (membrane/vesicle/protocell)
floors: coarse-grained continuum phase field (not a lipid bilayer); fixed periodic lattice
'membrane/vesicle/protocell/death' is analogy; not a cell; we did NOT make life
